name: NavigationBar
exportAliasNote: The `AppBar` runtime alias re-exports the family-level `<NavigationBar>` component (not just the home sub) so consumers can drop in `<AppBar variant="home|page|search">`. Declared on the home sub-spec because Home is the canonical default landing for `AppBar`-style consumers; the family-level export is what actually ships from packages/ui/src/index.js.
description: Landing-screen top bar — anchored to the top of a tab root (a feed, an inbox, a profile, a discover screen). A leading menu glyph + left-aligned title occupy the start of the row; up to three action icons (search, chat, profile / notifications) sit at the trailing edge. **The title slot defaults to the brand logotype rendered at 24px tall** (width auto, ratio preserved) so the landing rung of every Home tab opens with the product mark rather than a written page name. A string title is the fallback for screens that don't anchor a top-level brand identity (a secondary tab root, a feature surface, a dev screen); the fallback carries the system's largest page-level type rung (heading.lg, 24/Semibold) rather than the smaller drill-in rung used by Page, and shares the 24-tall rhythm of the logotype so the bar's height is unchanged across the two presentations.

title.description: Default: brand logotype `` rendered at a fixed 24px height (width auto). Fallback: plain string rendered as `heading.lg` text that truncates with ellipsis.

trailingActions.description: Up to three { icon, 'aria-label' } entries; laid out left-to-right with no inter-icon gap.

leadingIcon.description: Fixed — always the menu / hamburger icon (MenuIcon). Rendered as a transparent icon capsule with the 24px glyph centred.

title.description: Screen's identity. Default rendering is the brand logotype `` at a fixed 24px height (width auto, ratio preserved) so every Home tab opens with the product mark. Falls back to a plain-text string at heading.lg / Semibold / onSurface (truncates with ellipsis) when the screen has no logotype affordance.

trailingActions.description: Array of up to three icon actions at the end of the row. Each renders as a transparent icon capsule; capsules sit flush with no inter-icon gap.

sizing.viewportSafeArea: Bar's block-top padding stacks env(safe-area-inset-top) on top of container.xs so the surface fill extends through the device status-bar / notch zone — scrolled body content does not bleed through the transparent system chrome. Bar is the owner of the viewport-top inset; page shell MUST NOT re-pay it via its own padding-top.

states.note: Bar itself has no interactive state. Icon slots carry the standard Icon Button state recipe — default / hovered (sys.state.hover overlay) / pressed (sys.state.pressed overlay) / disabled / focused (three-layer focus ring). Title carries no states.

focusIndicator.description: The bar itself isn't a focus target. Its action slots (leading menu, trailing icons) inherit each control's own focus composition — Icon Button → Outward — so the ring belongs to whichever capsule the keyboard lands on. See the contained sub-components for the visual contract.
focusIndicator.trigger: :focus-visible (keyboard / programmatic focus, never plain mouse click)

behavior.pageChrome: Bar renders in the page flow at the top of the screen; the host page is responsible for pinning (position: sticky, etc.).
behavior.titleTruncates: Long page names truncate with ellipsis; the bar height stays at 56 regardless. Treat as a safety net — author concise titles.
behavior.trailingIconCeiling: Three is the conventional ceiling — a fourth action belongs in an overflow menu. The component does not enforce.

forbidden: brand color on the title / wordmark — header chrome stays on sys.color.surface; the wordmark paints sys.color.onSurface
more than three trailing actions — anatomy invariants cap at three
leading action different from the menu glyph — the home variant always opens with hamburger / menu
title bar height different from 56px — geometry is fixed across all navigation-bar subs
